title: Class 2 deterministic ordering — smoke_detected context override boosts CLASS_0 first
description: Deterministic scenario for the doc 12 §14 Phase 1.5 context-override mechanism. Same opt-in (input_mode='scanning' + scan_ordering_mode='deterministic'). The trigger is C206 (insufficient context) so the trigger-bucket priority would be [CLASS_1, CLASS_0, CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, SAFE_DEFERRAL]. BUT the input fixture has environmental_context.smoke_detected=true. The class2_scan_ordering_rules.context_overrides entry {if_field='environmental_context.smoke_detected', if_equals=true, boost_first='CLASS_0'} fires — CLASS_0 is moved to the front of the priority list. Result: the user hears '1/4. 긴급상황인가요?' first instead of the lighting prompt. scan_ordering_applied records the smoke_detected override having fired. Scenario asset only — does not authorize actuation.

steps.description: Publish a C206-triggering context payload with environmental_context.smoke_detected=true. Critically, the trigger itself is NOT an emergency (trigger_event remains a button event); the smoke signal is a context-only risk marker. Without the override mechanism, this would still route C206 → CLASS_2 with the trigger-bucket priority. Note: in production, smoke_detected=true also typically routes via the policy_router emergency-trigger path before reaching Class 2 — this scenario specifically exercises the case where the policy_router did NOT classify smoke as emergency yet (because, e.g., it doesn't pass the threshold predicate alone and the button event is what arrived first).

Class2ClarificationManager.start_session(input_mode='scanning', scan_ordering_mode='deterministic') runs. apply_scan_ordering reads the C206 bucket [CLASS_1, CLASS_0, CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, SAFE_DEFERRAL] then iterates context_overrides. The smoke_detected override fires → CLASS_0 boosted to position 0. Final priority list: [CLASS_0, CLASS_1, CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION, SAFE_DEFERRAL]. session.scan_ordering_audit.applied_overrides records the smoke fire.

Per-option utterances follow the post-override order: '1/4. 긴급상황인가요?' (the C3_EMERGENCY_HELP CLASS_0 candidate) FIRST, '2/4. <C1_LIGHTING prompt>', etc. The user hears the emergency option first because the system thinks risk is elevated.

User single_click on option 0 → emergency confirmation. Terminal CLASS_0. (The terminal selection itself is not what's specifically tested — what's tested is that the user heard the EMERGENCY option first because of the smoke context override.)
scan_ordering_applied.applied_overrides=['environmental_context.smoke_detected=True→CLASS_0']. final_order starts with the CLASS_0 candidate.

The integration verifier asserts (a) scan_ordering_applied.applied_overrides has at least one entry, (b) the first entry references smoke_detected, (c) final_order's first candidate has candidate_transition_target='CLASS_0', (d) terminal transition is CLASS_0 (because that's what the user picked).

transition_outcomes.condition: smoke_detected context override moves CLASS_0 to front of priority list. User hears emergency option first and confirms.
transition_outcomes.example_confirmation: single_click on option 0 (now the CLASS_0 emergency candidate due to smoke override)

notes: Context overrides are pure permutation — they move a transition_target to the front of the priority list, never inject new candidates (doc 12 §14.4 + §14.8 explicit boundary). If a target appears in the priority list as a result of the override but no actual candidate has that target, ordering simply doesn't move anything (defensive).
The smoke_detected override is one of three shipped examples; gas_detected → CLASS_0 and doorbell_detected → CAREGIVER work the same way.

Multiple overrides can fire simultaneously — later-declared overrides win the front spot (stack semantics). E.g., if both smoke AND doorbell were true, the doorbell override (later in the list) would put CAREGIVER first.

Even though the user heard CLASS_0 first and picked it, the terminal CLASS_0 transition still flows through the standard emergency handler — same authority path as any other CLASS_0 outcome. Ordering doesn't grant new authority.
An adjacent scenario family (P1.5 expansion candidate) could exercise doorbell_detected → CAREGIVER first override under C208 trigger — both the bucket and override agree there.
